006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.management;
018
019import java.util.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
022import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
025
026/**
027 * Mixin flavor of the {@code /loggers} runtime log-level management endpoint.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * Composes the logger read/set endpoints into a host resource via
031 * {@link Rest#mixins() @Rest(mixins=LoggersMixin.class)}.  Delegates to a shared {@link LoggersManager}
032 * worker so the mixin and {@link LoggersResource resource} flavors cannot drift.
033 *
034 * <p>
035 * The {@code GET} endpoints (list all / read one) are read-only and safe to expose; the {@code PUT}/{@code POST}
036 * set-level endpoint <b>mutates runtime logging</b> and should be guarded (deny-by-default) when assembled into a
037 * management group &mdash; see the actuator group's exposure policy.
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Backend:</h5><ul>
040 *    <li>Drives {@link JulLogBackend java.util.logging} by default; declare a Logback / Log4j2 backend
041 *       explicitly via {@link LoggersSettings.Builder#backend(LogBackend)} &mdash; see {@link LogBackend}.
042 * </ul>
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
045 *    <li class='jc'>{@link LoggersResource}
046 *    <li class='jc'>{@link LoggersManager}
047 *    <li class='jc'>{@link LogBackend}
048 * </ul>
049 *
050 * @since 10.0.0
051 */
052@Rest
053public class LoggersMixin extends RestMixin {
054
055   private final LoggersManager manager = new LoggersManager();
056
057   /**
058    * [GET /loggers] - All loggers and their configured levels.
059    *
060    * @param req The HTTP request.
061    * @return A sorted map of logger name to configured level (empty string = inherited).
062    */
063   @RestGet(
064      path="/loggers",
065      summary="Runtime logger levels",
066      description="Lists all loggers and their configured levels (empty = inherited from ancestor)."
067   )
068   public Map<String,String> getLoggers(RestRequest req) {
069      return manager.getLevels(req.getContext());
070   }
071
072   /**
073    * [GET /loggers/{name}] - One logger's configured level.
074    *
075    * @param req The HTTP request.
076    * @param name The logger name ("ROOT" for the root logger).
077    * @return The configured level name, empty string if inherited.
078    * @throws NotFound If no logger with that name is registered.
079    */
080   @RestGet(
081      path="/loggers/{name}",
082      summary="Runtime logger level",
083      description="Returns the configured level of a single logger."
084   )
085   public String getLogger(RestRequest req, @Path("name") String name) {
086      var level = manager.getLevel(req.getContext(), name);
087      if (level == null)
088         throw new NotFound("No logger named ''{0}'' is registered.", name);
089      return level;
090   }
091
092   /**
093    * [PUT /loggers/{name}] - Set a logger's level at runtime.
094    *
095    * <p>
096    * <b>Sensitive (mutating) &mdash; deny-by-default:</b> responds {@code 403} unless a {@link LoggersSettings}
097    * bean with writes enabled is registered.
098    *
099    * @param req The HTTP request.
100    * @param name The logger name ("ROOT" for the root logger).
101    * @param level The level name (e.g. FINE, INFO, OFF), or blank to inherit from the ancestor.
102    * @return The newly-configured level (empty string if inherited).
103    * @throws Forbidden If the set-level endpoint is not enabled.
104    */
105   @RestPut(
106      path="/loggers/{name}",
107      summary="Set logger level",
108      description="Sets the java.util.logging level of a single logger at runtime (blank to inherit).  Disabled by default; opt in via LoggersSettings."
109   )
110   public String setLogger(RestRequest req, @Path("name") String name, @Content String level) {
111      if (! manager.resolveSettings(req.getContext()).isWriteEnabled())
112         throw new Forbidden("The /loggers set-level endpoint is disabled.  Register a LoggersSettings bean with write enabled to use it.");
113      manager.setLevel(req.getContext(), name, level);
114      // setLevel creates the logger on demand, so getLevel always returns non-null here (level name or "" if inherited).
115      return manager.getLevel(req.getContext(), name);
116   }
117
118   /**
119    * [POST /loggers/{name}] - Set a logger's level at runtime (POST alias of {@link #setLogger(RestRequest,String,String)}).
120    *
121    * @param req The HTTP request.
122    * @param name The logger name ("ROOT" for the root logger).
123    * @param level The level name, or blank to inherit.
124    * @return The newly-configured level (empty string if inherited).
125    * @throws Forbidden If the set-level endpoint is not enabled.
126    */
127   @RestPost(
128      path="/loggers/{name}",
129      summary="Set logger level",
130      description="Sets the java.util.logging level of a single logger at runtime (blank to inherit).  Disabled by default; opt in via LoggersSettings."
131   )
132   public String setLoggerPost(RestRequest req, @Path("name") String name, @Content String level) {
133      return setLogger(req, name, level);
134   }
135}
